text: 100g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. everything else is fairly underwhelming. not relevant here.
0g of protein. the rest is not doing much. also not relevant here.
vitamin e at 19% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not adding much here.

pros first: helps meals actually work. a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one. efficient energy source. that can be a strength in the right context. small amounts can change a whole meal. that leverage matters when the fat quality is good.

on the weaker side: calorie density is brutally high. tiny serving errors matter here. extremely calorie dense by default. that is the unavoidable category burden. not a complete food on its own. even the better fats are still just part of a meal.
B tier. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
